mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate lower borrowing costs and higher valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over any 3-year rolling window, or the average return in periods of FEDFUNDS decline is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a rapidly deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:recessionary periods often follow sharp unemployment increases
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the average return in triggered periods is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous and significant decline in both industrial production and retail sales indicates a broad and severe economic slowdown, signaling reduced corporate earnings and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to widespread demand and supply shocks.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both INDPRO and RSAFS show month-over-month declines.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling significantly during a period of high interest rates indicates a severe demand shock in the housing market, which spills over to broader economic weakness and negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon as market participants react to the news.
evidence_refs: fact:housing market sensitivity to interest rates
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances during periods where FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation erodes purchasing power and corporate margins, leading to expectations of tighter monetary policy from the central bank, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as discount rates rise and earnings forecasts are cut.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 4%, or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in non-farm payrolls indicates a weakening labor market, signaling reduced consumer demand and corporate profitability, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in slower economic growth.
evidence_refs: fact:employment as a key economic indicator
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in more than 50% of months following a decline in PAYEMS, or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals a significant slowdown in manufacturing and overall economic activity, leading to immediate negative sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon as investors react to the implications for corporate earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production as a leading indicator of economic health
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A significant quarter-over-quarter contraction in real GDP indicates a broad economic recession, leading to sustained negative corporate earnings expectations and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as the market adjusts to the new economic reality.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP as the primary measure of economic output
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 50% of periods following a quarter-over-quarter decline in GDPC1, or the average return is not negative.
